title: Indulgent Mexican Tortilla Quesadilla
description: A buttery, crispy quesadilla with creamy mascarpone, melty cheeses, and a hint of cumin, perfect for a lazy Sunday dinner.
instructions: Slice each tortilla in half to create four smaller pieces for easier handling.
In a bowl, combine mascarpone, cheddar, mozzarella, and cotija to form a creamy cheese mixture.
Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat and add 10g butter, letting it melt and lightly brown.
Add diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet, sautéing until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in cumin, smoked paprika, and black pepper, cooking for 30 seconds to release aromas.
Spread the cheese mixture evenly over one tortilla half, then top with sautéed onion mixture.
Place another tortilla half on top, pressing gently to seal the edges.
Cook the assembled quesadilla in the skillet for 2-3 minutes per side, or until the bottom is golden brown and cheese begins to melt.
Flip carefully, add the remaining 10g butter to the pan, and cook the second side until golden and fully melted.
Remove from heat, let rest for 1 minute, then cut into wedges.
Garnish with fresh cilantro if desired, and serve hot.
